name: Detroit Communities Tax & Resource Center
description_short: Detroit Communities Tax & Resource Center provides free tax and financial assistance to low-income residents in the Detroit area, operating as a non-profit community resource.
description_long: Detroit Communities Tax & Resource Center is a community-based non-profit organization serving the Detroit metropolitan area, specifically operating out of Redford Township, Michigan. The organization focuses on providing accessible financial and tax preparation services to underserved populations who may lack resources or knowledge to navigate complex tax and financial systems. Based on the available information, the center operates during limited hours (weekday mornings and early afternoons, with Saturday afternoon availability) to accommodate working individuals and families seeking assistance. The center's primary mission appears to center on removing barriers to financial stability and tax compliance for low-income Detroit-area residents. What distinguishes Detroit Communities Tax & Resource Center is its commitment to providing these services at no cost, positioning itself as a genuine community resource rather than a commercial enterprise. The organization maintains a physical location that serves as a touchpoint for residents seeking in-person assistance, and their website presence through standard business directories indicates professional operation and accessibility. The limited hours of operation (9:30-15:00 weekdays, 12:00-16:00 Saturday) suggest a modest operation focused on direct service delivery rather than high-volume processing. An honest assessment reveals that without comprehensive website detail beyond basic directory information, potential clients may find it difficult to determine the full scope of services offered beyond the name reference to tax and resource assistance. The reliance on a basic Waze business listing rather than a robust dedicated website may limit visibility and detailed information for prospective clients seeking specific service details, eligibility requirements, or service scope.
address: 25245 Five Mile Rd ste 3W, Redford Township, MI 48239

pros: Free services with no fee for tax preparation or financial assistance
Non-profit organization structure ensures mission-driven service delivery
Physical in-person location available for direct client interaction in Redford Township
Extended Saturday hours (12:00-16:00) accommodate working individuals
Contact phone number provided for direct client inquiries and scheduling
Established community presence with consistent business hours and operation
Serves underserved low-income populations in Detroit metropolitan area
cons: Limited online presence with minimal information beyond basic directory listing
Restricted operational hours (closes at 3 PM weekdays, no Sunday hours) may not accommodate all work schedules
No detailed website describing specific services, eligibility requirements, or application process
Small operation may limit capacity to serve all community members seeking assistance
Lack of transparent service scope information makes it difficult for potential clients to self-assess fit before contacting
best_for: Low-income Detroit-area residents needing free tax preparation assistance
Individuals seeking financial counseling or resource navigation in the Detroit metro area
Working families who can access services during early afternoon or Saturday hours
Unbanked or underbanked community members needing foundational financial guidance
diagnosis: Detroit Communities Tax & Resource Center is best for low-income Detroit-area residents seeking free tax preparation and basic financial assistance. The main caveat is that extremely limited online information makes it essential to call directly (734-377-4712) to confirm specific services, eligibility requirements, and whether your needs fall within their service scope.
